mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 5% while housing starts decline, or out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 70% of observations during early-cycle phases, or return autocorrelation fully explains the pattern instead of INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and historically low unemployment indicates an overheating economy facing aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as markets price in sustained economic expansion.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2%, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns when payrolls exceed 300k but unemployment rises, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and manufacturing contraction, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 65% of sharp decline instances, or the signal is fully explained by concurrent FEDFUNDS movements rather than supply shocks.
